description.en: Question 1. Which measure best represents the distance from 0 to point N on the number line below? The number line from 0 to 1 is divided into 4 equal intervals, and N is at the first tick mark after 0. Choices: A: 1/6 unit; B: 1/5 unit; C: 1/4 unit; D: 1/3 unit.
Question 2. What number sentence is another way to represent the missing number in the equation 36 ÷ 4 = □? Choices: A: □ × 4 = 36; B: 36 × 4 = □; C: 36 + 4 = □; D: □ ÷ 4 = 36.
Question 3. What is another way of expressing 8 × 12? Choices: A: (8 × 10) + (8 × 2); B: (8 × 1) + (8 × 2); C: (8 × 10) + 2; D: 8 + (10 × 2).
Question 4. Which fraction goes in the blank to make a true number sentence? 4/8 > □. Choices: A: 4/6; B: 2/8; C: 7/8; D: 4/4.

Question 5. Sue is going to cover her kitchen floor with tiles that are each 1 square foot. The floor is in the shape of a rectangle that is 6 feet wide and 8 feet long. A diagram shows the rectangular floor divided into 6 rows and 8 columns of equal square tiles; the key says each tile equals 1 square foot. How many tiles are needed to cover the floor? Choices: A: 14; B: 24; C: 28; D: 48.

Question 6. What is the area, in square units, of the shaded part of the figure? A rectangular grid has 6 rows and 7 columns. The middle 4 rows and middle 5 columns form a shaded rectangle; the outside border of squares is unshaded. The key says each small square equals 1 square unit. Choices: A: 18; B: 20; C: 22; D: 42.

Question 7. The number of objects described in which situation can be represented by 24 ÷ 4? Choices: A: There are 24 boxes with 4 pencils in each box; B: There are 24 people on a bus, and 4 people get off the bus; C: There are 24 marbles that need to be sorted into 4 equal groups; D: There are 24 books on a shelf, and 4 more books are put on the shelf.

Question 8. The rectangular garden shown is divided into two regions—one for carrots and one for peas. Both regions are 5 square units high. The carrots region is 10 square units wide, and the peas region is 6 square units wide. The key says each small square equals 1 square unit. Which expression represents the area, in square units, of the whole garden? Choices: A: (5 + 10) + (5 + 6); B: (5 × 10) × (5 × 6); C: (5 × 10) + (5 × 6); D: (5 + 10) × (5 + 6).

Question 9. Which number represents the location of point P on the number line below? The number line from 0 to 1 is divided into 6 equal intervals, and P is at the second tick mark after 0. Choices: A: 2/7; B: 2/6; C: 3/7; D: 2/4.

Question 10. The Rogers family drove a total of 482 miles, starting on Friday and ending on Sunday. They drove 138 miles on Friday and 225 miles on Saturday. How many miles did they drive on Sunday? Choices: A: 119; B: 121; C: 363; D: 745.
